content: Onboarding wizard pitfalls in SaaS: (1) Static tours nobody finishes — linear click-next flows get skipped; they should be skippable with drop-off tracked. (2) Information overload — showing all features before user reaches first value causes abandonment; cap onboarding to the single activation action. (3) No personalization — developer vs marketer need different paths; role selection at start is table stakes. (4) Gate before value — email verification, mandatory profile, or complex setup before the user sees the product kills conversion; defer non-critical steps. (5) One-time-event mindset — treating onboarding as a checklist rather than an ongoing system means returning users get no contextual help. (6) Empty state generic messages — 'No data yet' feels broken; use sample data or guided prompts. (7) Onboarding staleness — wizard copy refers to features that moved or were renamed; tie wizard steps to feature flags so stale steps can be toggled off.
